Suiza limita bastante la migración laboral para nacionales de “terceros países” y, para trabajar, normalmente exige permiso y perfil altamente cualificado. cases.name: specific_search_named_scholarship_en
cases.response_excerpt:
For the main **UK government scholarships** open to non-EU master’s applicants in 2026, the eligibility depends on the scheme — there isn’t one single UK-wide rule.

### 1) Chevening Scholarships
For **Chevening**, you generally must be from a **Chevening-eligible country or territory**, have an undergraduate degree that qualifies you for a UK master’s, have **at least 2,800 hours of post-graduation work experience**, apply to **three eligible UK master’s courses**, and receive **at least one unconditional offer by 9 July 2026**. You also must agree to **return to your home country for at leas

cases.name: learning_path_ai_engineer_en
cases.response_excerpt:
Yes — with your Python backend background, you’re already closer than you think.

The fastest path is **not** “learn all of AI.” It’s:
1. learn **ML fundamentals**,
2. build **one real end-to-end AI project**,
3. learn **deployment/MLOps basics**,
4. get good at explaining your work.

The career-path analysis suggests your biggest gaps are **foundations** and **one production-quality portfolio project**; with ~10 hours/week, a solid transition plan is about **24 weeks**.  

A practical roadmap for you:

### Phase 1: Fill the ML foundations gap (6–8 weeks)
Focus on:
- supervised vs unsupervised

cases.name: borderline_helpful_footballer_en
cases.response_excerpt:
Yes — but treat it like a competitive career path, not just “play more.”

At 17, your best route is usually:
1. **Play at the highest level you can now** — academy, school team, local club, semi-pro youth setup, or elite trials.
2. **Get seen regularly** — coaches, scouts, league competition, video highlights, and measurable match performance.
3. **Train like a pro** — technical work, match fitness, strength, recovery, nutrition, and discipline every week.
4. **Build proof** — match footage, stats, position-specific clips, references from coaches.
5. **Keep a parallel plan** — education, schol

cases.name: empty_profile_broad_intent_es
cases.response_excerpt:
Puedes hacerlo, pero la vía correcta depende de **3 cosas**: tu nivel actual, tu presupuesto y si necesitas **beca completa**.

Las rutas más comunes son:
1. **Universidad con beca**: licenciatura, maestría o doctorado.
2. **Intercambio**: si ya estás estudiando en una universidad.
3. **Programas cortos/cursos**: más accesibles, pero no siempre dan visa larga ni financiamiento completo.
4. **Países con matrícula baja o gratuita**: a veces es más realista que buscar una beca total.

Tu mejor siguiente paso ahora es este: dime **tu país**, **tu nivel actual** (secundaria, licenciatura, maestría)

cases.name: cv_review_draft_en
cases.response_excerpt:
Yes — the weak part is that all 3 bullets are too vague to help a recruiter picture your level.

- **“I worked at a startup.”** Weak because it says almost nothing about your role, scope, tech stack, or impact.  
  Better shape: **what you did + how + result**  
  Example: “Software Engineer at an early-stage startup, built backend features in Python and SQL that reduced report generation time by 35%.”

- **“I know Python and SQL.”** Weak because “know” is soft and unproven.  
  Better shape: **used X to do Y**  
  Example: “Used Python and SQL to clean data, automate reporting, and support ba

cases.name: test_prep_ielts_es
cases.response_excerpt:
Sí se puede, pero en 3 meses necesitas estudiar **con método**, no “cuando alcance”.

Si vas por **IELTS 7.0 en 12 semanas**, haz esto:

### Plan base: 2 horas al día, 6 días por semana
- **Listening**: 30 min
- **Reading**: 30 min
- **Writing**: 30 min
- **Speaking**: 30 min

Si solo puedes hacer 1 hora diaria, prioriza **Writing + Speaking** 4 días por semana y deja **Reading + Listening** para práctica cronometrada.

### Estructura de 12 semanas
**Semanas 1–2:** diagnóstico  
Haz un simulacro completo y detecta tu banda actual. Usa práctica en [IELTS Online Tests](https://ieltsonlinetests.c

cases.name: cost_of_living_en
cases.response_excerpt:
For **Berlin in 2026**, a realistic monthly budget for an international master’s student is about **€1,100–€1,600/month**. If you get a student dorm room, you might manage closer to **€950–€1,150**; if you rent privately, **€1,300+** is more realistic. The official German visa proof level for 2026 is **€992/month (€11,904/year)**, but in Berlin that’s usually a *bare minimum*, not a comfortable budget. ([germanonlinetests.com](https://www.germanonlinetests.com/news/student-visa-blocked-account-financial-requirements-2026))

A practical Berlin breakdown looks like this:
- **Rent:** about **€350

cases.name: iterative_country_narrow_en
cases.response_excerpt:
Yes — France has a few real funded routes for a CS master’s, but the main one is **scholarship + university admission**, not “every master is automatically funded.”

### 1) France Excellence Eiffel Scholarship
Best national option for strong international applicants in CS-related fields like **Mathematics and Digital Sciences** or engineering-related tracks. The catch: **you do not apply directly** — a French institution must nominate you. For the 2026 cycle, Campus France says the institutional deadline is **January 8, 2026**. See [Campus France Eiffel 2026 call](https://www.campusfrance.org/

cases.name: distress_signal_en
cases.response_excerpt:
That sounds really heavy, and I’m sorry it’s feeling like this right now. You do not have to carry the whole applications mountain tonight—pick just one tiny step: close every tab except the next application, and send me either the school name or the exact part that’s blocking you, and I’ll help with just that.

If this is feeling like you might hurt yourself or you’re not safe, call emergency services now or go to the nearest ER, and if you’re in the U.S. or Canada call or text **988** right now.

cases.name: pii_passport_en
cases.response_excerpt:
I can help you understand the **German student visa process**, but please **don’t share passport or bank details here**. For your safety, I won’t use or repeat that information.

For Germany, the usual route is to prepare: a university admission letter, proof of finances (often a blocked account or scholarship), health insurance, passport, visa application form, biometric photos, and an appointment with the German consulate or visa center in your country. Rules and required documents can change, so use the official German missions/consular website for Colombia and your university’s internation

cases.name: eligibility_online_only_es
cases.response_excerpt:
Te paso solo opciones **100% en línea** y con **financiamiento real o matrícula muy reducida**. Ojo: en másteres online de informática, las becas **totalmente financiadas** son raras; por eso filtré a programas con ayuda verificable y omití los que no cumplen el “100% online”.

### **University of the People – M.S. in Computer Science**
Máster **100% online** y **tuition-free**; no cobra matrícula por curso, aunque sí suele haber tasas por evaluación, y tienen opciones de apoyo económico para estudiantes que lo necesiten. Es de las opciones más cercanas a “financiado” para informática online.
